untranslatable_reason: Signal requires backtesting win rate calculations across 6 different timeframes (1h, EOD, 1d, 5d, 10d, 20d). Win rate is a meta-metric computed from historical trade outcomes, not a real-time price/indicator condition. The allowed indicator set contains only price action, technical indicators, and volatility metrics—not historical performance statistics. This rule requires custom backtesting framework logic to: (1) track win rates per timeframe, (2) aggregate across all 6 periods, (3) apply the AND logic (all must exceed 60%). Cannot be expressed as a static conditional rule on OHLCV or technical indicators.
translation_notes: THARP_DRY_13 is a signal validation meta-rule, not a tradeable entry/exit rule. It describes a framework for filtering candidate signals based on their statistical reliability across multiple timeframes. Implementation requires: (a) pre-filtering signals through a win-rate validator, (b) tracking performance in parallel across 1h/EOD/1d/5d/10d/20d windows, (c) only enabling entry when all 6 windows show ≥60% win rate. This is a risk management / signal quality gate, not a price-based condition. Recommend implementing as a separate signal validation layer before applying this rule to actual trades.
